articles.title: How Trade Tariffs Are Raising the Cost of New Amusement Equipment for US Operators
articles.summary: Trade tariffs on imported amusement equipment are adding significant costs for US parks and FECs sourcing rides and attractions from European and Asian manufacturers. Beyond the catalogue price, tariffs compound existing expenses like shipping, installation, and theming, potentially pushing total project budgets even higher. FEC operators planning capital expenditures on new attractions should factor tariff exposure into their procurement and budgeting strategies.

articles.title: Enchanted Parks Deploys WhiteWater's Vantage Platform Across Eight Former Six Flags Properties
articles.summary: Enchanted Parks is rolling out WhiteWater's Vantage guest technology platform across eight US parks acquired from Six Flags, integrating ticketing, payments, loyalty, F&B, retail, and access control into a single ecosystem. The initiative is one of the most ambitious guest-tech integrations in the attractions industry and aims to deliver a fully personalized, data-driven visitor journey. FEC operators can look to this case study as a blueprint for unifying fragmented point solutions into a cohesive guest experience platform.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Opens in Frisco, Texas — A New Benchmark for Family Entertainment
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort has officially opened in Frisco, Texas after three years of development, featuring seven themed areas built around beloved children's characters. The resort raises the competitive bar for family entertainment destinations in the Dallas-Fort Worth metro area and beyond. FEC operators should study its programming, themed zones, and IP-driven experience design as consumer expectations for immersive family venues continue to escalate.

articles.title: Vintia Unifies Ticketing, POS, and Access Control for French Amusement Park Vulcania
articles.summary: Vulcania, one of France's top ten amusement parks with 400,000 annual visitors, has selected Vintia's Recreatex and Enviso platform to consolidate ticketing, booking, point-of-sale, and access control into a single system over a four-year rollout. The deployment is designed to streamline daily operations and deliver a more seamless guest experience. This is a strong case study for mid-to-large FEC operators evaluating all-in-one operational technology platforms.

articles.title: Majid Al Futtaim Opens Large-Scale Explorers FEC in Dubai Shopping Mall
articles.summary: Majid Al Futtaim has launched Explorers, a new family entertainment centre inside City Centre Mirdif in Dubai, positioning it as the emirate's only large-scale indoor kids destination combining adventure play with expert-led interactive workshops. The venue targets children aged 1–12 across three distinct themed zones covering soft play, sensory learning, and water-based activities. The concept illustrates the growing trend of edutainment-led FEC design in mall environments across the Middle East.

articles.title: The Breakers Resort Plans Upscale Family Entertainment Center — Renderings Revealed
articles.summary: Palm Beach's iconic Breakers resort has released renderings of a planned family entertainment center, signaling the continued expansion of FEC concepts into luxury hospitality settings. The project reflects a broader trend of high-end resorts integrating entertainment amenities to extend guest dwell time and on-property spending. FEC operators and designers should watch this development as an example of premium FEC positioning.

articles.title: LEVEL 99 Expanding to Connecticut with First West Hartford Location
articles.summary: LEVEL 99, the adult-focused competitive entertainment concept featuring dozens of physical challenge games, is opening its first Connecticut location in West Hartford. The brand's continued geographic expansion underscores strong consumer demand for active, competitive entertainment experiences beyond traditional bowling and laser tag. FEC operators should note LEVEL 99's growth trajectory as a benchmark for skill-based adult entertainment concepts.

articles.title: New Waterpark and Entertainment Complex Planned for West Des Moines
articles.summary: New development plans and renderings have been revealed for a waterpark and entertainment complex in West Des Moines, with the city recently approving a development agreement for the Grand Experience project. The mixed-use entertainment destination signals continued investor appetite for large-scale leisure developments in Midwest markets. FEC operators and investors in the region should monitor this project as a competitive development.

articles.title: West Des Moines Approves Development Agreement for Grand Experience Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: The city of West Des Moines has formally approved a development agreement for the Grand Experience, a planned waterpark and entertainment destination. Municipal approval clears a key hurdle for the project and brings it closer to groundbreaking. This development is relevant to FEC operators and investors tracking new large-format entertainment destinations entering Midwest markets.

articles.title: Wevr's Location-Based VR Experiences Surpass One Million Visitors Globally
articles.summary: Wevr reached over one million visitors across five location-based VR experiences launched in Asia, Europe, and South America during the past year, with each representing a world or regional first. The company's shared immersive storytelling model demonstrates that location-based VR continues to scale commercially when tied to compelling IP such as The Little Prince and historical sites. FEC operators evaluating VR attractions should study Wevr's approach to audience-driven, shareable spatial experiences.

articles.title: Altitude Trampoline Park Announces New Location in Litchfield Park, Arizona
articles.summary: Altitude Trampoline Park is expanding into Litchfield Park, Arizona, adding another unit to the national trampoline park franchise's growing footprint. The opening reflects continued demand for indoor active entertainment in the suburban Southwest market. Operators in the trampoline park segment should note Altitude's ongoing expansion as a sign of sector health and competitive market activity.

articles.title: Sky Zone Trampoline Park Brings New Jobs and Family Entertainment to Local Market
articles.summary: A new Sky Zone Trampoline Park has opened, bringing active family entertainment and new employment opportunities to its community. Sky Zone continues to be one of the most active franchise brands in the indoor active entertainment space. This opening reinforces the trampoline park category's continued growth and franchise viability for prospective FEC operators.

articles.title: Eternity Bowling to Showcase EV99 String Bowling System at Fun Asia Expo 2026
articles.summary: Eternity Bowling will exhibit its EV99 string bowling system at the Fun Asia Expo 2026 in Indonesia, giving operators across the Asia-Pacific region a hands-on look at the latest in string pin technology. String bowling systems have gained traction globally due to lower maintenance costs and improved lane economics compared to traditional free-fall pinsetters. FEC operators considering bowling as an anchor attraction should evaluate string pin systems as a capital-efficient alternative.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Launches in London as Part of £20M UK Expansion
articles.summary: US-based indoor golf and entertainment brand Five Iron Golf has opened its first London location as part of a £20 million UK expansion plan. The brand's international push highlights the growing global appetite for tech-enabled indoor golf experiences that combine simulators with food and beverage in an upscale social setting. FEC operators should watch Five Iron Golf's UK rollout as a signal of indoor golf's continued crossover into mainstream entertainment venues.

articles.title: InfoComm 2026: AV Industry Prioritizes Ease of Use Over Raw Performance Specs
articles.summary: At InfoComm 2026, the audiovisual industry signaled a shift toward simplifying complex systems for easier deployment and maintenance, even as display and projection capabilities continue to advance. This trend has direct implications for FEC and attraction operators who depend on AV systems for immersive experiences, digital signage, and entertainment programming. Simpler, more reliable AV infrastructure could reduce operational overhead and technical staff requirements for venue operators.

articles.title: Heat Wave Drives Families to Indoor Entertainment Venues, Boosting FEC Traffic
articles.summary: Businesses in the Greensboro, North Carolina area, including indoor entertainment venues, are reporting a surge in customers as a summer heat wave pushes families indoors seeking air-conditioned activities. This seasonal demand pattern is a recurring revenue opportunity for FEC operators who can effectively market their climate-controlled environments during extreme weather events. Operators should consider heat-wave-themed promotions and social media campaigns to capitalize on these traffic spikes.

articles.title: Triad-Area Indoor Entertainment Businesses Report Customer Surge During Heatwave
articles.summary: Indoor entertainment and amusement businesses across North Carolina's Triad region are seeing measurable increases in foot traffic as extreme summer heat drives families to seek indoor activities. The pattern reinforces the value of climate-controlled FEC venues as weather-resilient leisure destinations. FEC operators should use heat events as marketing triggers and ensure staffing and inventory are scaled to meet demand spikes.

articles.title: Jake's Unlimited FEC Finds New Direction Under Local Ownership
articles.summary: Jake's Unlimited, a large-format family entertainment center in Arizona, is experiencing a revival under new local ownership after a period of uncertainty. The transition highlights how locally invested operators can reinvigorate established FEC brands by reconnecting with their communities. This story offers relevant lessons for FEC operators navigating ownership transitions or brand repositioning.

articles.title: Alterface and Fantawild Partner to Develop Next-Generation Interactive Attractions
articles.summary: Belgian interactive attractions specialist Alterface has announced a partnership with Chinese theme park giant Fantawild to co-develop unique interactive attractions. The collaboration pairs Alterface's shooting and interactive ride technology with Fantawild's large-scale production capabilities and extensive park network. FEC and attraction operators should watch this partnership as it may produce new interactive ride formats relevant to smaller entertainment venues.

articles.title: Zamania by Leo & Loona Opens as New Indoor Entertainment Venue at Dubai's Ibn Battuta Mall
articles.summary: Zamania by Leo & Loona has opened at Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai, adding another branded indoor entertainment concept to the UAE's growing FEC landscape. The opening reflects the Middle East's continued investment in mall-based family and children's entertainment destinations. FEC operators and suppliers eyeing regional expansion should take note of the UAE's active entertainment development pipeline.

articles.title: Parky's FEC at Moorestown Mall Back on Track After Lawsuit Resolution
articles.summary: Parky's, a planned family entertainment center at Moorestown Mall in New Jersey, is moving forward after a lawsuit that had stalled the project was resolved. The resolution clears the path for the FEC to proceed with development inside the mall, illustrating both the legal complexities that can arise in retail-adjacent entertainment projects and the resilience of mall-based FEC development. Operators and developers in the mall FEC space should note the importance of thorough lease and partnership agreements.

articles.title: Boxing Arcade Machines Gaining Traction in Modern Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Boxing arcade machines are increasingly appearing in FECs, bars, and entertainment venues as operators seek physical, high-energy arcade experiences that drive repeat play and social engagement. The format appeals to competitive players and groups, making it a strong complementary piece to traditional redemption and video game arcades. FEC operators evaluating arcade floor refreshes should consider boxing and strength-test machines as crowd-drawing additions.

articles.title: Fiesta Village FEC Closes After Decades of Operation in Colton, California
articles.summary: Fiesta Village, a long-running family entertainment center in Colton, California, has closed its doors, drawing large crowds for a final send-off before shutting down. The closure is a reminder of the ongoing challenges facing legacy FEC operators in competitive or economically shifting markets. Operators should view closures like this as opportunities to study what succession planning, reinvestment, or repositioning strategies might have extended the venue's viability.

articles.title: Michigan's Adventure Celebrates 70th Season with Free Admission for Guests 70 and Older
articles.summary: Michigan's Adventure is marking its 70th anniversary season by offering free admission to guests aged 70 and older on select weekdays through its new 70 Plus Club program. The promotion is a creative loyalty and community engagement initiative that drives weekday traffic and generates positive media coverage. FEC operators can draw inspiration from milestone-based promotions as a way to build community goodwill and fill off-peak capacity.

articles.title: Museum of Ice Cream's New Las Vegas Flagship Offers Lessons in Experiential Entertainment Design
articles.summary: Museum of Ice Cream has opened a new flagship location at Area15 in Las Vegas, offering a case study in how experiential entertainment venues blur the line between immersive attraction and social media content destination. The venue's appeal across multiple age groups — from young children to teenagers and adults — reflects the growing demand for experiences that work for the whole family without feeling designed exclusively for one demographic. FEC designers and operators can draw insights from its multi-zone, shareable-moment approach to guest experience.

articles.title: Universal Orlando Launches $199 Unlimited-Day Florida Resident Ticket for Two Parks
articles.summary: Universal Orlando Resort is offering Florida residents a new $199 unlimited-access ticket covering both Universal Studios Florida and Universal Islands of Adventure through December 16. The aggressive resident pricing strategy is designed to drive repeat visitation and local loyalty in a highly competitive Central Florida market. FEC operators should monitor how major parks use tiered resident pricing to build visit frequency, a strategy adaptable to local membership and pass programs.
